answer:
The vertical tangent for a polar curve occurs when \(\frac{dx}{d\theta} = 0\) and \(\frac{dy}{d\theta} \neq 0\).  
For \(r = \sin 3\theta\), using \(x = r\cos\theta\), the derivative condition simplifies to \(\cos 3\theta \cos\theta - 3\sin 3\theta \sin\theta = 0\).  
Solving numerically in the first quadrant gives \(\theta \approx 0.39\), which corresponds to option B.  

\boxed{B}
